artPrompt: A wiry goblin rogue slouched against a rain-slick alley wall in the wide frame, a deadly glint in his eye, one hand resting near a hidden blade, amber lamplight grazing his cheek while his coat bleeds into ink shadow below. Cigar smoke and three years of waiting hang in the smoky-brown haze. All coiled, patient menace. Intimate criminal mood, rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Wiseguy with a Grudge
text:
Deathtouch
Whenever an opponent who owes you a Favor casts a spell, Wiseguy with a Grudge gets +1/+1 until end of turn. He remembers. He always remembers. It's his whole thing.
flavorText: "You said you'd call. That was three years ago. I waited by the phone."
